title: Special ward to treat heatstroke patients set up at Cuddalore GH - thehindu.com
full_text:
As mercury levels continue to rise in Cuddalore, arrangements have been made at the Cuddalore Government General Hospital to treat members of the public complaining of heat-related stress.
According to an official, six beds have been readied for heatstroke patients in the Intensive Medical Care Unit in the hospital. The ward has two ventilators and also has separate restrooms and bathrooms.
Patients arriving in emergency units with symptoms such as heatstroke, severe dehydration, or related conditions are being immediately referred to the special ward, where doctors are prepared to provide treatment in line with established medical guidelines. Besides, dispensing stations have been set up across the campus for distribution of Oral Rehydration Solution (ORS) sachets, he said.
Doctors said heatwaves in India often see temperatures crossing 40 degrees Celsius, increasing the risk of illnesses. Children below 12 years, pregnant women, senior citizens above 60, and those with kidney or heat ailments are more vulnerable. They have been advised to avoid going outdoors between 9 a.m. and 4 p.m.
In case of heatstroke symptoms, the public has been advised to seek immediate treatment at nearby primary health centres or government hospitals.
